page: Microsoft Azure D160alds_v7 is a virtual machine instance from the Daldsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 160 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 80 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor using the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 320.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. Storage is provided locally with a capacity of 9449 GB, and the instance is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. No dedicated GPU hardware is included in this configuration.

Benchmark results place the CPU performance of this instance in the top tier. In stress-ng testing, the single-core CPU performance scored 3791.58, while the multi-core CPU performance reached 287625.19, both ranking in the top 10% of tested systems. Single-threaded gzip compression achieved a throughput of 41.4 GB/sec. Decompression benchmarks using bzip3 registered at 18.07 GB/sec for single-threaded operations and scaled to 999.55 GB/sec in multi-threaded operations, reflecting high parallel processing efficiency.

The instance offers high compute density and substantial local storage capacity, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. However, the 2.0 GB of memory per vCPU represents a tradeoff, as memory-intensive applications may experience bottlenecks despite the high core count. This configuration is suitable for compute-optimized tasks, large-scale data decompression, parallel processing workloads, and applications requiring high-capacity local storage.
description: Microsoft Azure D160alds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Daldsv7 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 160 dedicated vCPUs and 80 physical cores. It features 320.0 GB of system memory, establishing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio, and includes 9449 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 instance delivers top-tier CPU performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 287625.19 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 999.55 GB/sec. The low memory-to-core ratio represents a design tradeoff, prioritizing raw compute density and local storage capacity over memory volume. This configuration is suited for compute-intensive parallel processing, large-scale data decompression, and workloads requiring substantial local disk space without high memory demands.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D160alds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 160 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 320 GB of RAM, and 9449 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core processing performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D160alds_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine with 160 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 320 GB RAM, and 9449 GB local storage for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A high-core compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and massive local storage for intensive parallel processing workloads.

320.0 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio
9449 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier multi-core performance in stress-ng and bzip3 benchmarks
Designed for compute-intensive parallel processing and data decompression
